summary: Split the later portion of gm_1601 into a new gm_16AE translation unit so lbl_8046B6A0 becomes the start of that file's .bss section. The PR updated GALE01 split ranges, build configuration, moved the 0x8016AE38-0x80174274 code and associated statics into gm_16AE.c/gm_16AE.static.h, and adjusted nearby files that depended on those statics. The author explicitly framed this as an infrastructure/file-layout fix to restore matches and enable more matching, separated from future matching work because the bot would report moved functions as broken in the old unit.
what_changed: Added a new split entry for melee/gm/gm_16AE.c in config/GALE01/splits.txt with .text 0x8016AE38-0x80174274, .rodata 0x803B7A60-0x803B7B18, .data 0x803D5620-0x803D6488, .bss 0x8046B6A0-0x8046DBE8, .sbss 0x804D65A0-0x804D65B8, and .sdata2 0x804DA298-0x804DA300.
Shrank gm_1601.c's split ranges so it ends at 0x8016AE38 and its .bss ends at 0x8046B6A0, making lbl_8046B6A0 the first BSS symbol in the new gm_16AE file.
Added Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gm_16AE.c") to configure.py so the new source participates in the build.
Moved the functions beginning at gm_8016AE38 and many placeholder/nonmatching declarations from gm_1601.c into the new gm_16AE.c.
Created gm_16AE.static.h containing the moved static data declarations, struct definitions, and local prototypes needed by the new unit, including lbl_803D5A4C and lbl_8046B6A0.
Reduced gm_1601.static.h by removing the statics now associated with gm_16AE and marking several remaining rodata-like arrays as const with zero initializers.
Changed gmresult.c and gmresultplayer.c to include gm_16AE.static.h instead of gm_1601.static.h.

Added several static declarations to gm_17C0.c, including lbl_803D9D20_t and BSS/data-like placeholders such as lbl_80472C30, lbl_80472CB0, lbl_80472D28, lbl_80472E48, lbl_80472EC8, lbl_80473594, lbl_80473700, and gm_804771C4.
smart_moves: Treated BSS section anchoring as a file-layout problem rather than trying to force local matches inside the wrong translation unit.

Split the work into a separate PR before adding more matches, acknowledging that the decomp bot would report moved matched functions as broken in gm_1601 even though they reappear as matches in gm_16AE.
Updated the split file, source files, static headers, and configure.py together, avoiding a half-split state where symbols or build objects would be inconsistent.
Kept the new unit nonmatching in configure.py, preserving current project status while enabling later matching work.
Moved dependent static declarations into the header consumed by gmresult.c and gmresultplayer.c instead of leaving those files tied to gm_1601.static.h after the ownership boundary changed.
decomp_lessons: When a global/static BSS symbol must be emitted at the beginning of a file's .bss section, the correct fix may be to split the translation unit at the corresponding text/data boundary.
For decomp.dev match accounting, file splits can look like equal numbers of broken and new matches; reviewers should distinguish movement between units from true code regressions.
A split should adjust all section ranges, not only .text: this PR changed .rodata, .data, .bss, .sbss, and .sdata2 boundaries for gm_1601 and gm_16AE.
Static headers need to follow symbol ownership after a split; consumers such as gmresult.c and gmresultplayer.c should include the static header for the unit that now owns the data they reference.
Moving large initializer tables such as lbl_803D5A4C can be necessary to keep data section ordering consistent with the new file boundary.
naming_conventions: The new file name gm_16AE.c follows the address-based gm_* convention, using the start address 0x8016AE38 abbreviated as 16AE.
The companion private header is named gm_16AE.static.h, matching the existing gm_1601.static.h pattern for file-local declarations used during decomp.
Unidentified functions retained address-based names such as gm_8016AE38, fn_8016B138, and fn_8016F1F0.
Unidentified globals retained lbl_ address names such as lbl_8046B6A0, lbl_803D5A4C, and lbl_804D65A0.
Known lifecycle-style suffixes visible in moved placeholders, such as _OnFrame, _OnEnter, and _OnLoad, were preserved rather than renamed in this split.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a translation-unit split at 0x8016AE38 to align compiler-emitted section starts with the target binary.
Made lbl_8046B6A0 the first BSS declaration in gm_16AE.static.h so the new file's .bss starts at 0x8046B6A0.
Kept many functions as /// # placeholders while moving them, minimizing semantic edits and isolating the layout change.
Retained M2C_FIELD-based accesses in the moved code for unresolved struct fields, avoiding risky refactors during a layout-only PR.

The bot reported 38 new matches in main/melee/gm/gm_16AE and 38 corresponding broken matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1601, consistent with relocation of already-matching functions rather than functional changes.

review_feedback.feedback: lbl_8046B6A0 needs to be the start of a file's bss section, so the split fixes matches and enables continued matching.
The author intentionally issued this as a separate PR from future matches because the decomp bot would interpret file-split movement as broken functions.

Reported 38 new matches in main/melee/gm/gm_16AE and 38 broken matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1601, plus one unmatched-function improvement for fn_8016B388 in gm_16AE and many apparent unmatched regressions in the old gm_1601 unit.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match functions in gm_16AE after lbl_8046B6A0 was made the BSS start
Search for additional PRs that split gm files due to BSS or data section boundary constraints
Check whether gm_16AE.static.h include guard __GALE01_1601C4 was later renamed or adjusted
Look for future renames of lbl_8046B6A0_t and its unk fields after this split
Compare decomp.dev match reports for PRs where functions move between units to distinguish real regressions from file-split artifacts
